entities.name: Geometric Distance Model
entities.context: Framework viewing career transitions as movement in a 2D space of role and domain, where distance correlates with market risk

entities.name: Career Mobility
entities.context: The ability to transition between roles and industries, governed by perceived risk and pattern matching

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's career coaching company; domain context for this position

entities.name: Pivot
entities.context: A career transition that changes one variable (role or domain) while keeping the other stable

entities.name: Diagonal Jump
entities.context: A high-risk career move that changes both role and domain simultaneously; market cannot price it

entities.name: Pattern Matching
entities.context: Mechanism employers and investors use for risk mitigation when evaluating candidates

entities.name: Information Asymmetry
entities.context: The gap in knowledge between candidate and market that increases with distance from established expertise

entities.name: Degrees of Freedom
entities.context: Framework borrowed from physics to calculate probability of career transition success

entities.name: Sequential Pivoting
entities.context: Strategy of moving into new domain with old role first (or vice versa) rather than changing both at once

relationships.context: Career mobility is governed by geometric distance from existing domain and role
Market uses pattern matching to assess risk; distance increases information asymmetry
Pivot changes one variable; diagonal jump changes both and is riskier
Career coaching should help candidates sequence pivots rather than attempt diagonal jumps
Career strategy viewed as degrees of freedom to calculate transition probability
Changing both role and domain creates disconnection from value-chain and increases asymmetry
